copy.title: Tigers destroy Rays. White Sox blank Twins. Knicks face elimination tomorrow.
copy.keyTakeaway: Baseball's having a day—Tigers, White Sox, and Mets all dominated—while the stock market lost its footing on tech weakness. Everything's pointing toward a reset week with the NBA Finals, Monaco Grand Prix, and World Cup opening in eight days.

todaysHits.sports: Three teams absolutely buried their opponents. No drama.
todaysHits.markets: Tech got hammered. SPY flat. NVDA down 3.6%.
todaysHits.golf: Brian Campbell leads Memorial. Tournament underway.
todaysHits.f1: Monaco this weekend. All eyes on street circuit.
todaysHits.culture: PlayStation State of Play grabbed headlines. Gaming's moment.

lead.headline: White Sox just ran a shutout clinic on Minnesota
lead.whatHappened: Chicago walked into Minnesota and blanked the Twins 8-0. Detroit also crushed Tampa Bay 7-2, and the Mets dismantled Seattle 7-1. Three games, three blowouts. The AL Central is starting to look like a two-horse race.
lead.whyBullet1: The White Sox shutout means their pitching staff just showed up in a division where Minnesota thought they were competitive.
lead.whyBullet2: When you're getting outscored 15-2 across your last two games, you're not sneaking into October—you're watching it on TV.
lead.whatToSay: If the Twins don't tighten up fast, Chicago just told them exactly who's running the Central.

sportsOther: White Sox just reminded Minnesota that some teams show up to play baseball.
Mets walked into Seattle and made it look easy.

markets.mood: Tech got hammered today as the 10-year yield jumped 0.8%, making expensive growth stocks look worse by comparison — NVDA down 3.6%, MSFT down 3.2%, but the broader market held up thanks to defensive plays and bond money rotating out.
markets.whyBullet1: Your 401k's tech-heavy index funds took a real hit today, but the 10-year climbing to fresh highs means bond positions are finally competitive again — time to check if your allocation actually matches your risk tolerance.
markets.whyBullet2: Watch for Fed speakers Thursday and Friday — if they signal more hawkish moves, yields could keep climbing and drag down growth names harder; if they sound dovish, you'll see a snap-back rally.
markets.bringUp: Bitcoin got crushed down 5.6% today while stocks only fell 0.7%, which is wild because crypto's supposed to be uncorrelated — turns out when the real rate environment shifts, everything sells off together.

golf.headline: Campbell, Coody, Fox tied atop Muirfield Village heading into weekend
golf.whyCare1: Memorial is a FedEx Cup points bonanza and the PGA Tour's most prestigious regular-season event — winning here matters more than most tournaments.
golf.whyCare2: Muirfield Village is a graveyard for weak ball-strikers; whoever controls the short grass this weekend wins.
golf.watchFor: Whether Campbell can hold the lead through 54 holes — he's been quietly consistent but hasn't closed out major tournaments lately.
golf.whatToSay: Campbell's playing well, but Muirfield always exposes you if you're not hitting it pure. That course doesn't care who you are.

f1.headline: Monaco's tight streets demand perfection. Max wants repeat.
f1.whyCare1: Monaco separates the true racers from the rest—one mistake ends your weekend.
f1.whyCare2: Verstappen's hunting another win to extend his championship lead over the field.
f1.watchFor: First-lap chaos in the narrow tunnel section. That's where overtakes die and collisions happen.
f1.whatToSay: Monaco's the race where you can't just muscle your way through—you have to be surgical.

culture.topic: PlayStation State of Play drops new trailers
culture.whatHappened: Sony held a PlayStation State of Play presentation in June 2026 showing off upcoming games and announcements.
culture.whyItMatters: If you game or know someone who does, this is where Sony signals what's actually coming to PS5 next.
culture.whatToSay: Did you catch the State of Play? Anything in there worth your time?

culture.topic: Nick Herbig signs long-term extension with Steelers
culture.whatHappened: The Pittsburgh Steelers locked up edge rusher Nick Herbig to a lucrative long-term deal.
culture.whyItMatters: The Steelers are betting on Herbig as a cornerstone pass rusher, which means they're serious about competing now.
culture.whatToSay: Herbig just got paid by the Steelers. That's a guy they clearly believe in.

culture.whatHappened: Adrien Brody as a Hungarian-Jewish architect who arrives in America after WWII and tries to build something real.

copy.finalSharpTake: Baseball went full blowout today—Tigers, White Sox, and Mets all won by five runs or more, which means someone's pitching staff got completely shredded and the other team's just ran away with it. Nothing sexy about that, but it does tell you the margin for error is gone heading into a World Cup month where every game matters and every miss costs you. Meanwhile your tech stocks got hammered again (NVDA down 3.6, MSFT down 3.2) while bonds climbed, which is the classic flight-to-safety move right before something bigger shifts. Knicks-Spurs Game 2 tomorrow night is going to feel small by comparison, but at least you know someone's actually playing for something.

glance.sports: Tigers crushed the Rays 7-2, White Sox shut out the Twins 8-0, and the Mets demolished Seattle 7-1 — baseball's having a day.
glance.market: SPY down 0.7%, but the real pain: NVDA -3.6%, MSFT -3.2%, BTC -5.6% — big tech and crypto took the hit.
glance.bestConvo: The White Sox just blanked Minnesota. That's the kind of pitching performance you don't forget, and it matters for playoff positioning.
glance.watchNext: NY-San Antonio Game 2 tomorrow in the NBA Finals — the Knicks need to steal one on the road or this series gets away fast.
glance.quickRec: Brian Campbell's leading the Memorial Tournament at even par; if you've got skin in the game this week, he's the name to watch.
